div.head:
No. 356
Editorial Note
div.p: Following the fourth formal meeting with President Truman, Prime Minister Churchill went to New York, January 9–11, and then to Ottawa for discussions with the Canadians, January 12–15. A report on the talks in Canada is in telegram 114, infra. The Prime Minister then returned to Washington, January 16–18, to address a joint session of Congress on January 17 and for a final meeting with President Truman, January 18. For a record of this final meeting, see Document 359.

Foreign Secretary Eden remained in Washington until January 10 before leaving for New York where he made an address at Columbia University the next day. Eden stayed in New York 4 more days before flying back to the United Kingdom on January 15.
